radius_measurements.notes: R from van Belle 1999 relation using Absil 2009 excess-corrected theta_LD 2.223 mas + Davis 2005 f_bol. Davis 2005 (uncorrected) gives a smaller R 1.744 / hotter Teff 8819.

inclination_measurements.notes: VLTI/AMBER Br-gamma spectro-astrometry: projected spin-axis PA only; i_star unconstrained (>15 deg floor). PA ~ debris-ring PA.

disk_measurements.morphology: narrow eccentric ring (e=0.11) with sharp inner edge
disk_measurements.notes: HST/ACS coronagraphy first resolved the main ring; eccentricity e~0.11; sharp inner edge interpreted as gravitational sculpting by an unseen shepherd body

disk_measurements.morphology: narrow eccentric ring, width Delta_a/a~0.13, e=0.11+/-0.01; vertical opening angle 1.0+/-0.25 deg from midplane (exponential scale height)
disk_measurements.notes: ALMA Cycle 0 millimeter imaging; constrains shepherd planet mass to < 3 M_Jup; refines geometry of the main ring. aspect_ratio h=H/r=0.0175 derived from the reported vertical opening angle 1.0+/-0.25 deg from the midplane (h=tan(theta)); the only vertical-structure measurement for this disk (MacGregor 2017 used a 2D model and only cites this).

disk_measurements.morphology: narrow eccentric ring; complete ALMA map
disk_measurements.notes: MacGregor et al. 2017 'A Complete ALMA Map of the Fomalhaut Debris Disk': canonical mm geometry — inclination 65.6 +/- 0.3 deg, PA 337.9 +/- 0.3 deg, argument of pericenter 22.5 +/- 4.3 deg, e 0.12 +/- 0.01, inner edge 136.3 +/- 0.9 AU, width 13.5 +/- 1.8 AU (first detection of apocenter glow). Radii 133-158 AU retained from Boley et al. 2012 / Kalas et al. 2005 and e=0.11 from Kalas 2005 (all within the literature spread of MacGregor's 136.3 / 0.12). The inner disk + intermediate belt are apsidally misaligned with this ring (PA ~329.6 deg, Gaspar 2023). (Audit fix: the previous 'White et al. 2017 / 2017ApJ...842...80W' citation was wrong — that bibcode is an unrelated Fermi pulsar paper; the 336.28/38.04 PA/omega were mis-sourced.)

disk_measurements.morphology: broad drawn-out inner disk; deprojected outer boundary ~77 AU (periapsis) to ~96 AU (apoapsis), truncated by the inner gap at ~78 AU. Inner limit ~10 AU is the saturation edge. Inclined ~47.8 deg at PA 329.6 deg, strongly misaligned with the ~65.6 deg / PA 337.9 deg outer cold ring (the headline JWST result). Not a narrow asteroid-belt analog; PR-drag-fed continuous emission rather than a discrete warm ring.
disk_measurements.notes: JWST/MIRI 15.5 and 23 um imaging revises the pre-JWST SED-inferred 8-15 AU / ~150 K narrow warm component (Su 2013) into a broad inner disk. outer_radius_au set to the ~78 AU inner gap (deprojected boundary swings 77 AU periapsis to 96 AU apoapsis) so it no longer overlaps the intermediate belt. Inner-disk fit: inclination 47.8 deg, PA 329.6 deg (Gaspar 2023) -- significantly misaligned with the 65.6 deg outer cold ring (MacGregor 2017), the headline JWST finding. The earlier 67.52 deg / 336.28 deg recorded here was the outer-ring deprojection orientation, NOT the inner-disk fit. Radial values are profile extents; no formal fit-parameter error bars in the paper.

disk_measurements.morphology: eccentric narrow belt; semi-major-axis edges a~83 AU (e=0.31, inner) to a~104 AU (e=0.265, outer), width 7-20 AU, PA ~329.6 deg (co-oriented with the inner disk), misaligned (apsidal) relative to the outer cold ring. Inner gap at ~78 AU separates it from the broad inner disk; outer gap ~104-134 AU separates it from the main cold ring. Likely shepherded by an unseen planet.
disk_measurements.notes: JWST/MIRI resolves a previously unknown eccentric intermediate belt. inner/outer_radius_au are the deprojected semi-major-axis edges a~83 and a~104 AU (Gaspar 2023); the earlier ~60-110 AU figure was the eccentric periapsis-apoapsis swing, which read as a false overlap with the inner disk when drawn as a plain annulus. Eccentricity e~0.3 and apsidal misalignment vs. the main cold ring argue for resonant sculpting by an unseen intermediate planet.

disk_measurements.morphology: main cold ring, resolved at 15.5 and 23 um
disk_measurements.notes: JWST/MIRI mid-IR imaging of the full multi-belt system; geometry parameters of the main ring not re-derived since ALMA (White 2017) is the canonical mm geometry source. Gaspar 2023 inclination 67.52 deg adopted for deprojection consistent with White 2017's 65.6 +/- 0.4 deg.

disk_measurements.morphology: extrasolar Kuiper-belt analog; cold component SED fit
disk_measurements.notes: Herschel/PACS far-IR imaging of the cold main ring; SED fit yields T_dust ~ 65 K; canonical dust-temperature source for the cold ring

disk_measurements.morphology: mm-cm grain population of the main cold ring
disk_measurements.notes: SONS legacy survey submm photometry; main-ring dust mass ~1.5e-2 M_Earth in mm-cm grains; lower-bound imaged-grain mass (collisional cascade modeling implies 4-10 M_Earth in planetesimal reservoir)

sources.title: Butkevich & Lindegren (2014): Relativistic aspects of stellar kinematics
SIMBAD Astronomical Database (Wenger et al. 2000)
used_for: astrometry (ra, dec, parallax, pmra, pmdec, rv)

meta.notes: Stellar Phase 2 curated 2026-05-30 under curation-data-contract (Tier 1 inversion fix). Mass/radius/Teff/luminosity/age all anchored on Mamajek 2012 (2012ApJ...754L..20M), the dedicated age paper that re-derives the fundamental parameters and all values are cache-verified from its text + Table 2: mass 1.92 +/- 0.02 Msun (mean of 4 evolutionary-track sets, protosolar composition), R 1.842 +/- 0.019 Rsun and Teff 8590 +/- 73 K (van Belle 1999 relation from Absil 2009 theta_LD 2.223 +/- 0.022 mas + Davis 2005 f_bol), L 16.63 +/- 0.48 Lsun (bolometric, Davis f_bol + van Leeuwen 2007 parallax), age 440 +/- 40 Myr (weighted mean of Fomalhaut A isochronal 450 +/- 40 and the TW PsA companion's gyro/Li/X-ray ages 400 +/- 70). RADIUS/Teff DIVERGENCE: Davis et al. 2005 give a smaller R 1.744 / hotter Teff 8819 because they did NOT subtract the resolved circumstellar K-band excess; Absil 2009 corrected for it (theta_LD 2.223 mas), so the Mamajek/Absil values are preferred. The angular diameter is mildly oblate (1.021, Absil 2009), gravity darkening is mild (not pole-on like Vega). [Fe/H] near-solar: Mamajek assumes protosolar (Z 0.017, motivated by the solar metallicity of companion TW PsA); Dunkin 1997 measured -0.03; A-star spectroscopic [Fe/H] is scattered/unreliable (Erspamer 2003 +0.43). ROTATION: only v sin i ~93 km/s (Royer 2007 compilation, 85-100 km/s spread); NO measured photometric period for the A-star (the 10.3 d period belongs to the K-dwarf companion TW PsA) -- not recorded. ACTIVITY: log R'HK is not physically defined for an A4 V star (no convective dynamo) and no standalone coronal X-ray detection of the A primary exists -- intentionally not recorded.
